topbar.aboutDescription: A web application for managing security assessments, attestations, and compliance workflows using the CycloneDX Attestations (CDXA) standard. Supports standards import, evidence management, and CycloneDX BOM export.

setup.welcomeDescription: Let's set up your instance. You'll create an administrator account, import assessment standards, and then you'll be ready to go.
setup.connectivityNotice: Internet connectivity is required. Assessment standards will be imported from the CycloneDX standards feed during setup.
setup.createAdminDescription: This account will have full access to manage the application.
setup.importStandardsDescription: Importing standards from the CycloneDX standards feed. These standards define the requirements used for assessments.
setup.feedError: Unable to fetch standards feed. Check your internet connection.
setup.setupCompleteDescription: Your administrator account has been created and {count} assessment standards have been imported. You are signed in and can start managing assessments.

validation.usernamePattern: Only letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, and underscores

claims.predicateDescription: A predicate is a statement or condition that can be evaluated as true or false.

signatures.title: My Signatures
signatures.empty: You have not added any signatures yet. Click Add Signature to create one.
signatures.confirmDelete: Delete signature "{label}"? Past attestations signed with it remain valid.
signatures.label: Label
signatures.labelHint: A short name you will use to pick this signature at sign time.
signatures.typeImmutable: The signature type cannot be changed after creation.
signatures.name: Full Name
signatures.signedNameHint: The name rendered as the typed signature. Defaults to Full Name if blank.
signatures.legalIntentPlaceholder: I intend to be legally bound by this signature.
signatures.algorithmHint: Name must match the identifier recognized by Node crypto, for example RSA-SHA256 or sha256 for ECDSA.
signatures.publicKeyHint: Paste the PEM encoded public key. Private keys are never accepted.
signatures.certificateChainHint: Optional. One or more concatenated PEM certificates that establish trust for the public key.
signatures.uploadHint: PNG, JPEG, or SVG. Kept small; typically under 50 KB.
signatures.confirmRemoveImage: Remove the signature image from this record?

signAttestation.title: Sign Attestation
signAttestation.noSignatures: You have no stored signatures. Add one in Settings before signing an attestation.
signAttestation.signedNameHint: Defaults to the value stored on the signature. Override only if signing under a different rendered name.
signAttestation.digitalExplainer: For digital signatures the server returns a canonical hash you must sign with your private key. Paste the resulting signature value below.
signAttestation.prepareHint: The server returns the canonical SHA 256 hash of the attestation payload. Sign that hash with your private key, then paste the result.
signAttestation.canonicalHashHint: Sign this hash with your private key to produce the signature value.
signAttestation.signatureValuePlaceholder: Paste the base64 encoded signature value here.
signAttestation.signatureValueHint: The signature your local tool produced over the canonical hash.

progress.title: Progress
progress.createAssessmentToStart: Complete an assessment to see it here.

gettingStarted.welcomeDescription: Assessors Studio helps you evaluate organizations, teams, and products against industry standards, then produce formal attestations of conformance. Here is a quick overview of how it works.
gettingStarted.entitiesDescription: An entity is anything you want to assess: an organization, a team, a product, or a software component. You link each entity to the standards it needs to meet, such as ASVS, BSIMM, PCI DSS, or SSDF. Entities connect to each other to form a flexible hierarchy that mirrors your org structure or supply chain.
gettingStarted.assessmentsDescription: An assessment evaluates one entity against one standard. Assessors work through each requirement, recording a result and rationale. Evidence such as documents or test reports can be attached to support those findings. Multiple assessments can run in parallel across different standards.
gettingStarted.attestationsDescription: When an assessment is complete, you generate a formal attestation that records the results as a signed declaration. Attestations export as CycloneDX documents or PDF reports. Over time, the Progress view shows how conformance scores trend across assessments, helping you measure improvement.
gettingStarted.automationDescription: Assessors Studio is API first. Assessments, evidence collection, and attestation generation can all be orchestrated through the API, supporting human driven workflows, fully automated pipelines, or a mix of both.

projectDashboard.noAssessmentsWithDates: No assessments with dates to display.

webhooks.noDeliveries: No delivery records found for this webhook.
webhooks.regenerateHint: Generate a new signing secret (invalidates the current one)
webhooks.eventTypesRequired: At least one event type is required
webhooks.secretWarning: Copy this secret now. It will not be shown again in full.
webhooks.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete the webhook "{name}"? All delivery history will be removed.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: View and test external service connections

smtp.title: Email / SMTP
smtp.notConfiguredHint: SMTP is not enabled. Configure SMTP environment variables to enable email notifications.
smtp.setupGuideIntro: Add the following environment variables to your deployment to enable email notifications:

storage.title: Evidence Storage
storage.setupGuideIntro: Evidence files are currently stored in the database. To use S3-compatible object storage instead, add the following environment variables:
storage.awsExample: Set S3_BUCKET, S3_REGION, and credentials. No endpoint override needed.
storage.minioExample: Set S3_ENDPOINT to your MinIO URL and enable S3_FORCE_PATH_STYLE=true.
storage.doExample: Set S3_ENDPOINT=https://REGION.digitaloceanspaces.com and S3_REGION to your Spaces region.

webhook.title: Webhooks
webhook.enabledHint: Webhook delivery is active. Manage individual webhooks from the Webhooks page.
webhook.disabledHint: Webhook delivery is disabled. Set WEBHOOK_ENABLED=true to enable.

slack.title: Slack
slack.enabledHint: {count} integration(s) configured. Manage Slack integrations from the Chat Integrations page.
slack.disabledHint: Slack notifications are disabled. Set SLACK_ENABLED=true to enable.
slack.setupGuideText: Set the SLACK_ENABLED environment variable to true, then create incoming webhooks in your Slack workspace under Apps > Incoming Webhooks.

teams.title: Microsoft Teams
teams.enabledHint: {count} integration(s) configured. Manage Teams integrations from the Chat Integrations page.
teams.disabledHint: Teams notifications are disabled. Set TEAMS_ENABLED=true to enable.
teams.setupGuideText: Set the TEAMS_ENABLED environment variable to true, then create incoming webhooks via your Teams channel Connectors or the Workflows app.

mattermost.title: Mattermost
mattermost.enabledHint: {count} integration(s) configured. Manage Mattermost integrations from the Chat Integrations page.
mattermost.disabledHint: Mattermost notifications are disabled. Set MATTERMOST_ENABLED=true to enable.
mattermost.setupGuideText: Set the MATTERMOST_ENABLED environment variable to true, then create incoming webhooks in your Mattermost instance under Integrations > Incoming Webhooks.

metrics.title: Prometheus Metrics
metrics.enabledHint: Metrics endpoint is active at /metrics. Scrape it with Prometheus or any compatible collector.
metrics.disabledHint: Metrics endpoint is disabled. Set METRICS_ENABLED=true to enable.
metrics.setupGuideText: Set METRICS_ENABLED=true to expose the /metrics endpoint. Optionally set METRICS_TOKEN to require bearer token authentication for the endpoint.

chatIntegrations.title: Chat Integrations
chatIntegrations.subtitle: Manage Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Mattermost notification channels
chatIntegrations.name: Name
chatIntegrations.channelNameHint: Optional display name for admin reference. Does not affect delivery.
chatIntegrations.deleteConfirm: Delete integration "{name}"? This will also remove all delivery history.
chatIntegrations.slackSetupGuide: Create an incoming webhook in your Slack workspace: Apps > Incoming Webhooks. Copy the webhook URL and paste it below.
chatIntegrations.teamsSetupGuide: Create an incoming webhook in your Teams channel: Connectors > Incoming Webhook (or use the Workflows app for newer deployments).
chatIntegrations.mattermostSetupGuide: Create an incoming webhook in Mattermost: Integrations > Incoming Webhooks.

encryption.passthroughDesc: No master key is configured. Secrets are stored in plaintext. Configure MASTER_ENCRYPTION_KEY and restart to enable envelope encryption.
